Patch Set 2: (1 inline comment)

Running #2 with the rest of the HSM stack, calling hsm_release on an already released file causes a stack dump to the console from mdt_handle_last_unlink().

 # dmesg -c > /dev/null
# cd /mnt/lustre
# lfs setstripe -c2 f0
# dd if=/dev/urandom of=f0 bs=1M count=100
# lfs hsm_archive f0
# sleep 10
# lfs hsm_state f0
f0: (0x00000009) exists archived
# lfs hsm_release f0
# dmesg -c
# lfs hsm_release f0
# dmesg -c
LustreError: 9201:0:(mdt_lib.c:686:mdt_handle_last_unlink()) No need in LOV EA upon unlink
Pid: 9201, comm: mdt_rdpg00_001 Tainted: P           ---------------    2.6.32-358.11.1.el6.lustre.x86_64 #1
Call Trace:
 [<ffffffffa069b8cc>] ? mdt_handle_last_unlink+0x2bc/0x3f0 [mdt]
 [<ffffffffa06b42dc>] ? mdt_close+0x69c/0xac0 [mdt]
 [<ffffffffa068b177>] ? mdt_handle_common+0x647/0x16d0 [mdt]
 [<ffffffffa06c5885>] ? mds_readpage_handle+0x15/0x20 [mdt]
 [<ffffffffa0dfe158>] ? ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x398/0xc60 [ptlrpc]
 [<ffffffffa09c354e>] ? cfs_timer_arm+0xe/0x10 [libcfs]
 [<ffffffffa09d4a7f>] ? lc_watchdog_touch+0x6f/0x170 [libcfs]
 [<ffffffffa0df5569>] ? ptlrpc_wait_event+0xa9/0x290 [ptlrpc]
 [<ffffffff81055ab3>] ? __wake_up+0x53/0x70
 [<ffffffffa0dff4dd>] ? ptlrpc_main+0xabd/0x1700 [ptlrpc]
 [<ffffffffa0dfea20>] ? ptlrpc_main+0x0/0x1700 [ptlrpc]
 [<ffffffff81096936>] ? kthread+0x96/0xa0
 [<ffffffff8100c0ca>] ? child_rip+0xa/0x20
 [<ffffffff810968a0>] ? kthread+0x0/0xa0
 [<ffffffff8100c0c0>] ? child_rip+0x0/0x20

Patch Set 2:

Jumping into the conversation late, but I think it makes sense to skip release/restore for files that have no data on them.  At best this means an empty (sparse) file in the archive, but it is also possible to consume a lot of space in the archive writing out zeroes for no benefit.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

>Jumping into the conversation late, but I think it makes sense to skip release/restore for files that have no data on them. At best this means an empty (sparse) file in the archive, but it is also possible to consume a lot of space in the archive writing out zeroes for no benefit.

This is an ARCHIVE issue, not related to RELEASE. If you want to avoid that, set a specific policy for it in Robinhood to prevent such file to be archived, but, please, do not relate this to RELEASE.

Patch Set 3: (1 inline comment)

I've found an issue with Changelog and this patch.
When changelog is active, the transaction declared for release goes up to ~1400 credits.

Lustre: lustre-MDD0000: changelog on
Lustre: 25007:0:(osd_handler.c:828:osd_trans_start()) lustre-MDT0000: too many transaction credits (1494 > 1024)
Lustre: 25007:0:(osd_handler.c:835:osd_trans_start())   create: 8/200, delete: 0/0, destroy: 0/0
Lustre: 25007:0:(osd_handler.c:840:osd_trans_start())   attr_set: 0/0, xattr_set: 12/168
Lustre: 25007:0:(osd_handler.c:847:osd_trans_start())   write: 66/924, punch: 16/64, quota 2/2
Lustre: 25007:0:(osd_handler.c:852:osd_trans_start())   insert: 8/136, delete: 0/0
Lustre: 25007:0:(osd_handler.c:857:osd_trans_start())   ref_add: 0/0, ref_del: 0/0

Patch Set 3:

this isn't a big issue, rather a warning. to be seen only on a local setup where the filesystem (so the journal) are tiny.

You were right Alex, thanks. MDSJOURNALSIZE made the trick.

Patch Set 5:

This now needs rebase in sanity-hsm.sh. I don't really understand why this adds the tests that it does, since we don't have a copytool at this point. I see how it uses hsm_set to get around this but even when it runs and the tests pass, I see untrapped errors in the test logs.

It seems that all of the changes to sanity-hsm done here are included in or superseded by http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/6913.

Patch Set 5:

You were quicker than I :). I've made a new HSM stack based on latest master and I also realized this.

I think the simplest thing to do is to drop all changes in test #1, #2 and #3 from this patch. We will handle this in another patch (#6913).

parents.subject: LU-3643 ofd: get data version only if file exists

commit.subject: LU-1333 hsm: Add hsm_release feature.
commit.message:
LU-1333 hsm: Add hsm_release feature.

HSM Release is one of the key feature of HSM. To perform HSM
release, clients need to acquire the file lease exclusivelt and
flush dirty cache from clients. A special close REQ will be sent
to the MDT to release the lease and get rid of OST objects.

Signed-off-by: Aurelien Degremont <aurelien.degremont@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I77eeccf81a852955354a382d80b887df00d1c4fc
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7028
Tested-by: Hudson
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
